original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults, young people, and children with cellulitis or erysipelas.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Initial and follow-up assessment, antimicrobial review, reassessment, and referral decisions.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting cellulitis extent, site, severity, exposure and microbiology context, reassessment, worsening, severe pain, systemic illness, and clinician decisions. It does not diagnose infection, prescribe antibiotics, or generate referral.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.8 — alternative causes, extent, site, severity, exposure, and review context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting skin redness and swelling extent, site, severity, broken skin, penetrating or water exposure, microbiology already obtained, skin-tone visibility, and treatment already prescribed without generating diagnosis or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.9–1.1.12 — reassessment and microbiology review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, lack of improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, spread, underlying conditions, serious alternative diagnoses, and microbiology results during clinician reassessment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.13–1.1.14 — clinician referral and specialist advice criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-identified serious illness, periorbital location, unusual exposure, treatment non-response, lymphangitis, oral-intake limitation, and an already made referral or specialist discussion; it does not generate urgency.

title: Cellulitis and erysipelas: antimicrobial prescribing
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
population: Adults, young people, and children with cellulitis or erysipelas.
intended_setting: Initial and follow-up assessment, antimicrobial review, reassessment, and referral decisions.
